issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline. The focus ring remains invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content visible in the layout and does not clip it. This fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382-393: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. Screen reader only text must use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clipping.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transition for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses background-color: #5588bb with color: #ffffff. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and 3:1 for large text. Users with low vision cannot read the button text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global a selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. While some specific link styles provide hover underlines, not all do (such as social links), meaning some links are only distinguishable by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring entirely, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is active.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This breaks focus visibility for users relying on high-contrast system settings.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without adding a visible alternative. This hides focus state for the mobile navigation toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Icon buttons are difficult to tap accurately on mobile devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width and height of 28px with no padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. This is below the 44px minimum, making it hard to tap.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 436 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling this transition for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: rotateY/rotateX and transition: transform. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 831 is empty, so motion is not suppressed.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses words together. This reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. outline-offset on a zero-width outline renders nothing, removing the focus ring for keyboard users. This blocks keyboard-only users from identifying the focused element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No box-shadow or border is provided as a replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link's :focus-visible state removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the logo.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when this control is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's :focus-visible rule overrides the :focus box-shadow with outline: none and no replacement. Keyboard users lose the focus indicator when tabbing to this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 741: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 529: .btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum size for touch targets. This is a critical barrier for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-lg min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Large buttons should be easily tappable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: .btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets 20px dimensions, below the 24px absolute minimum. Icon buttons are frequently missed by touch users at this size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 606: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has a height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Padding is inside the height (border-box), so the hit area is small.
issuesFound.fix: Line 636: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This makes them difficult to tap on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 666: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Submenu items are often harder to target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Text inputs should be large enough for easy entry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, below the 44px recommended size. Icon buttons in forms should be easily tappable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 749: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Toggles should be easy to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1056: .filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1143: .pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1198: .product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links have a height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for optimal touch access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1473: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have a height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1479: .social-links a { min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible to all users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 467 is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 467: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform (rotate/perspective) and transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 806 contains empty rules and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 806: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 263: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). Many links (e.g., in .faq-item dd p) rely solely on color to distinguish them from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 272: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. There is no underline or other non-color differentiator, making them hard to identify for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1464: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; } footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. They lack an underline or other non-color differentiator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1476: .social-links a { text-decoration: underline; } .social-links a:hover { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users. The forced-colors media query exists but fails to restore focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367-370: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible base rule
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will see no focus indicator on any interactive element unless overridden by specific component rules.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which tightens text spacing significantly. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision, and may cause words to overlap at larger font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 182: word-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); /* 0em */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline alternative. The transition properties on .btn do not include a visible focus state, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 529-531: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector applies transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with transition: transform var(--transition-slower). This spatial movement lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override in the empty @media block at lines 1102-1107, which fails to disable the transform or transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102-1107: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 578: Line 578-579: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global anchor rule
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. While some specific links (feature cards, skip links) provide underline on hover, navigation links in the header, footer, and account areas rely solely on colour change (from #64748b to #5588bb or similar), which is indistinguishable for colour-blind users. No non-colour differentiator (font-weight, border-bottom, background) is provided for these critical navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR for specific navigation links add border-bottom or background-color change on hover/focus that is not colour-dependent.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px with no visible alternative. Although the card has a hover state with underline, the focus state is invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102-1104: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on mobile nav toggle

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form inputs

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) which is not overridden in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1379. issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body text

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 selector sets letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Headings already use tight line-height; combined negative letter-spacing reduces legibility, especially at large sizes.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1785: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the addDescribedBy call

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state when the menu opens, even though the close function sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen reader users via the liveRegion utility, unlike other interactive components in the codebase.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1247: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 1267: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals dropdown panels but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent accessibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the panel

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands FAQ answer panels and manages visibility but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", leaving the attribute incorrect when items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2785: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not move focus to drawer content
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Keyboard users cannot immediately access the navigation links without manually tabbing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after revealing the drawer

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active state visually via CSS classes but do not expose the pressed state to assistive technologies. The function should set aria-pressed="true" on the active filter button and "false" on others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2390: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field contains an error, so users may not be notified of the validation state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function adds the open class and announces the state change but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch that confuses assistive technology about the menu's status.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function updates the UI and `aria-expanded` state but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region. The `open` function announces its state, so the lack of symmetry prevents screen reader users from confirming the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` function removes the `hidden` attribute and adds classes but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger. The corresponding `_closeInstance` function sets it to "false", resulting in an incorrect ARIA state for the open dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` function changes visible content (opens the panel) but does not announce the state change to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the dropdown menu has opened.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` function hides the panel and updates `aria-expanded` but does not announce the state change to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the dropdown menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the panel and announces the action but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function sets it to "false", causing a state mismatch for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close` function collapses the panel and updates `aria-expanded` but does not announce the state change to the live region. The `open` function announces its action, so the lack of symmetry prevents screen reader users from confirming the answer has collapsed.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email field validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds inline validation to the email field using inlineValidator.bind(), which relies on srUtils.markInvalid. Because markInvalid does not set aria-invalid, screen readers do not announce the invalid state when email validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3902: Ensure srUtils.markInvalid is updated per Issue 3, or add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` in the _renderResult function of inlineValidator when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2228 (in _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after emitting the custom event. Line 2242 (in _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after emitting the custom event.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed (if not tabs)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons use aria-selected, which is correct for tab elements, but if these are visually styled as toggle buttons rather than tabs, they should use aria-pressed to indicate the active state. The code treats them as tabs with roving tabindex, but if the UI presents them as a binary toggle (month/year), aria-pressed is more appropriate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2893: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` if the elements are buttons, or ensure they have role="tab" if using aria-selected.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher missing aria-live announcement for system preference changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes their OS color scheme preference while the page is open, the themeSwitcher updates the data-theme attribute but does not announce the change to screen readers because the _mqlHandler only updates if the current theme is "system". Users who manually selected a theme won't be notified of system changes, and even system-mode users don't get an announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4486 (in _mqlHandler): Add `liveRegion.announce('Color scheme changed to match system preference');` when updating the theme based on system changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and removes the hidden attribute from the overlay, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2118: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_lockScroll();` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Only the _closeInstance function sets the attribute to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2225: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds the error message element to aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as being in an invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1646: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null check and before the `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` call.

issuesFound.title: contactForm fields validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds inline validation to multiple fields (full_name, email, subject, message, consent). When validation fails, aria-invalid is not set on these fields due to the srUtils.markInvalid deficiency, preventing screen reader users from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3970-3975: Apply the fix from Issue 3 to srUtils.markInvalid, or manually set aria-invalid in the inlineValidator._renderResult function when result.valid is false.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step fields validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard binds inline validation to step fields using inlineValidator. Invalid fields are not marked with aria-invalid="true", so screen reader users cannot identify which fields need correction when step validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4125-4130: Ensure srUtils.markInvalid sets aria-invalid="true" as described in Issue 3.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). The open() function does not call _trap.activate(), and close() checks if _trap exists before deactivating. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer when it opens, allowing focus to move outside to background elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2106 (in init function): Initialize the trap with `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });`. Line 2119 (in open function): Insert `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`. Line 2155 (in close function): Insert `_trap.deactivate();` before unlocking scroll.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls initialization check
issuesFound.explanation: While the code checks and sets aria-expanded if missing, and sets aria-controls if the drawer has an id, it does not verify that the drawer element actually has an id before attempting to set aria-controls. If _drawer.id is empty, aria-controls is not set, breaking the programmatic relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2093: Change condition to `if (_drawer && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || 'main-nav'); }` ensuring an id is always assigned to the drawer and referenced.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips remove button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The remove button (X) on filter chips is a toggle action that removes the filter. While it has an aria-label, it does not indicate its pressed state or action completion. However, since the chip disappears immediately when clicked, the removal is confirmed by the disappearance and the live region announcement from productFilter.
issuesFound.fix: No change required, or add `aria-pressed="false"` initially if maintaining a toggle state, but removal is acceptable.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function in the `productFilter` module toggles the `is-active` CSS class on filter buttons to indicate selection, but it fails to update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users relying on state changes will not know which filter is currently active, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2278: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module update the visibility of dropdown panels and emit custom events, but they do not call `liveRegion.announce`. Users of assistive technologies are not informed when a menu opens or closes, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1663: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 1682: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner appearance is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in the `cookieConsent` module dynamically inserts a dialog into the DOM and traps focus, but it does not announce the banner's appearance via the live region. Users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3703: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie consent banner displayed');`

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in the `statCounterAnimator` module updates `textContent` repeatedly to animate numbers. The element lacks `aria-live`, and the `aria-label` is only set upon completion. Screen readers will not announce the counting progression or the final value change reliably, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3510: `el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state when the menu opens, violating WCAG 4.1.2. The close() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Both _openInstance() and _closeInstance() fire custom DOM events but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users receive no feedback when dropdown menus open or close, particularly problematic when opened via keyboard shortcuts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after the panel is revealed. Line 1305: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after aria-expanded is set to false in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the menu is open. The paired _closeInstance() correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() helper only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid. The markValid() function does remove aria-invalid, creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the start of the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function stores a reference to a focus trap (_trap) but never calls activate() on it. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the overlay. The close() function deactivates the trap, implying it should be activated here.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: Insert `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` after the _lockScroll() call and before the emit() call in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: Undefined SELECTORS.navDrawer and SELECTORS.primaryNav
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() references SELECTORS.navDrawer and SELECTORS.primaryNav (line ~1220), but these keys do not exist in the SELECTORS object. This causes the drawer element query to fail (qs(undefined)), breaking the mobile navigation functionality entirely for keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 200 (SELECTORS object): Add `navDrawer: '#main-nav',` and `primaryNav: '#main-nav',` to the SELECTORS object, or change line 1220 to use `SELECTORS.mainNav`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module removes the `hidden` attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but it fails to set the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. The corresponding `_closeInstance` function correctly sets it to `"false"`, creating a state mismatch where the button reports "collapsed" while the menu is visibly open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it does not set the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. The `close` function correctly sets it to `"false"`. This causes screen readers to announce the answer as collapsed even when it is visually expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab sync function missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function within the `productFilter` module toggles the visual `is-active` class on filter buttons to indicate selection, but it does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Keyboard users relying on state attributes will not know which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module toggles the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes (`is-open`) but does not explicitly manage the `aria-hidden` attribute on the drawer element itself in the `open` and `close` functions. If the CSS does not enforce `display: none` when closed, the menu content may remain in the accessibility tree.

fpTitles: Undefined SELECTORS.navDrawer and SELECTORS.primaryNav

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, this function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1894: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state change to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2324: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Unlike the open() function which announces "Navigation menu opened", the close() function does not announce "Navigation menu closed". Users are not informed when the drawer closes, especially if focus does not move to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2344: Before the closing brace, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not reset to "false". This leaves screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2344: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, prices update visually across all plan cards, but screen readers are not informed of the content change via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3634: After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Since the drawer is closed by default, screen readers cannot convey the collapsible nature of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2363: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes, this function removes the error description but does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false", leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1906: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after removing describedby references.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". The register() function sets initial aria-expanded="false", but the toggle functions never update it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2516: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not reset to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2531: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item expands, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". While initial parsing sets the attribute, dynamic toggling does not update it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4124: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item collapses, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 4149: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer.mount() missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the suggestion list is hidden on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3283: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filter criteria change, the visible product count updates and cards animate, but no announcement informs screen reader users how many results match the current filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3844: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products found\`);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users activate the scroll-to-top button, the page scrolls and focus moves, but no confirmation is announced to screen readers that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2693: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens but focus is not trapped within it. Users can tab out of the drawer into the background content while it remains open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. The _trap variable exists but is never instantiated with createFocusTrap().
issuesFound.fix: Line 2324: After `_open = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate that a menu has appeared and how many items it contains.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2516: After opening the panel, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` (or similar appropriate string).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, no announcement confirms the action to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2531: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses inlineValidator which calls srUtils.markInvalid. As identified in Issue 9, this fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields, preventing screen readers from identifying errors.
issuesFound.fix: (Fix Issue 9 in srUtils to resolve this)

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses inlineValidator which calls srUtils.markInvalid. As identified in Issue 9, this fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: (Fix Issue 9 in srUtils to resolve this)

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form uses inlineValidator which calls srUtils.markInvalid. As identified in Issue 9, this fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: (Fix Issue 9 in srUtils to resolve this)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1685: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not notified when an item opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button. The initial state is set in register, but subsequent toggles are not reflected to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1838: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1850: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1290: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not toggle aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions manage drawer visibility via CSS classes but do not explicitly toggle aria-hidden on the _drawer element. If the CSS uses visibility or transform instead of display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1685: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
Line 1705: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the _countBadge text content when filters change but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match their filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2470: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays when the billing period changes but does not announce the new period or prices to screen readers. Users are not informed that the visible prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products"). The emit() call is insufficient for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing \${pageEntries.length} of \${totalMatches} products\`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_animating = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization missing aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the mobile navigation trigger button is queried but not initialized with aria-expanded="false" or aria-controls pointing to the drawer element. This prevents assistive technologies from understanding the relationship and initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2205: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`.
  Line 2206: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after verifying _drawer exists.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but fails to synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. The attribute is only initialized in register() but never updated when state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2305: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 2325: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` after clearing the describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion controller toggles answer panels via the open() and close() functions but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the question trigger buttons. While initialized in init(), the attribute becomes stale after the first toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3105: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function after `item.open = true;`.
  Line 3135: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the close function after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `mobileNav` module removes the open class but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `mobileNav` module closes the drawer but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to screen readers via the live region, unlike the `open` function which does announce.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function within the `mobileNav` module does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. It should be set to "false" by default.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module shows the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module hides the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within `dropdownMenu` emit custom events but do not write to the `liveRegion` to announce the dropdown state change to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `faqAccordion` module expands the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions within `faqAccordion` do not announce the expansion or collapse of the answer panel to screen readers via the live region.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function adds `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` utility function removes `aria-describedby` references but fails to remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce result count
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product count badge and empty state but does not announce the number of results found to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop onClick does not announce action
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick` function within `scrollToTop` scrolls the page to the top but does not announce "Scrolled to top of page" to screen readers via the live region.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent onAccept does not announce action
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept` function within `cookieConsent` grants consent and hides the banner but does not announce the acceptance to screen readers via the live region.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent onDecline does not announce action
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onDecline` function within `cookieConsent` revokes consent and hides the banner but does not announce the decline to screen readers via the live region.

issuesFound.title: pricing updateSavingsMessage does not use live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage` function updates text content dynamically to show savings but the element is not configured as a live region, nor is an announcement made. Screen readers may miss the update.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey validation state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1716: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles the mobile navigation drawer open by adding CSS classes and emitting events, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the menu trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens a dropdown menu panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, so screen readers do not announce that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2289: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2187: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion announcer, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the final emit call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error description association but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (set to "false" or remove it), leaving the field marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1728: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization code selects the trigger button and drawer elements but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, violating the requirement that toggle buttons reflect their initial closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2252: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after the event listeners are attached to the trigger.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable intended for focus trapping but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()`. Consequently, focus is not trapped within the drawer when open, allowing users to tab out of the modal-like interface into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2248: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true, onDeactivate: () => close() });` and Line 2168: Insert `_trap.activate();` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes a dropdown menu panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands an FAQ answer panel and animates its height, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function sets it to false, the open state is never communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3895: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which filter is currently active, failing to convey the toggle state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after calculating isActive.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens an FAQ item but does not announce the expansion to screen readers, so users are not informed that new content is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3912: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes an FAQ item but does not announce the collapse to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3933: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function filters the product grid and updates the count badge, but does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255: Insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function switches billing periods and updates prices visually, but does not announce the change (e.g., "Switched to annual billing") to screen readers via the liveRegion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3665: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger in register(), but the _openInstance and _closeInstance functions fail to toggle this state to "true" or "false" respectively. Screen readers will not announce the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); // In _openInstance after inst.open = true
  Line N: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); // In _closeInstance after inst.open = false

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); // Add to markInvalid function before addDescribedBy

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); // Add to markValid function

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter updates the visible card count and grid content dynamically but only emits a custom event. No text is written to an aria-live region, so screen reader users are not informed of the result count change.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); // Add to applyFilters after _updateCountBadge

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function announces "Navigation menu opened", but the close function does not announce "Navigation menu closed". This inconsistency leaves screen reader users unsure if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); // Add to close function

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox but aria-expanded is not explicitly set to "false" during initialization. It is only set when show() or hide() is called, potentially leaving the initial state undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // Add to siteSearch.init after mounting listbox
