issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid do not manage aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation functions mark fields as invalid by linking error messages via aria-describedby, but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` in markInvalid. Line 1315: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`) in markValid.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update their aria-expanded state when panels are opened or closed. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2256: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance. Line 2270: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that the navigation state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` in open(). Line 2185: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` in close().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not dynamically update aria-expanded when items are expanded or collapsed via user interaction. Although initialised correctly, the attribute becomes stale after the first toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3160: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open() function. Line 3185: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing dropdown menus does not announce state changes via the live region. Users navigating with screen readers are not informed when menu panels open or close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 2275: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (monthly/annual) updates prices visually but does not announce the change via the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have updated or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2945: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the display.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion listbox is shown or hidden. Screen readers cannot convey whether suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2608: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _open(). Line 2615: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _close().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The collapsed state of the suggestion listbox is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch element does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle switch (month ↔ year) has role="switch" but does not update aria-checked when toggled. The _toggleEl element requires aria-checked to communicate its state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2917: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` after calculating isYear.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer and focus does not move to the drawer content. Keyboard users can tab behind the modal overlay, creating a focus management failure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` to move focus into the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers are registered without initialising aria-expanded="false". The attribute is only set implicitly if the HTML has it, but the JavaScript should ensure it matches the initial hidden state of the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the updated result count is displayed visually but not announced to screen readers. Users do not receive feedback that filtering has completed or how many products are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3045: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches));` inside the filterAnimation.run onComplete callback.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action completion to screen readers. Users are not informed that they have returned to the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after scrolling completes.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button element (selected via SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn) is not provided with an aria-label or accessible text content. Screen readers announce it only as "button" without indicating its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: Add `setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop);` after selecting the button element.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1713: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1733: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function finds the trigger element but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the initial closed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Screen reader users will not know if the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1923: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1936: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2658: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1436: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not remove aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to false). Fields may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1453: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce result count via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product count badge and visibility but does not announce the new count (e.g., "5 products found") to screen readers via a live region. Users are unaware the filter results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2253: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all price displays when the billing period changes but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") via a live region. Screen reader users miss the context of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2483: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-checked or aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates visual classes for the billing toggle and period buttons but does not update aria-checked (for the switch) or aria-pressed (for the buttons). The state change is not reflected in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2523: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));
  Line 2528: if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick does not announce action via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce "Scrolled to top" via a live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2123: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in the srUtils module adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markInvalid after addDescribedBy): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown's expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance after inst.panel.removeAttribute): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in _closeInstance after removeClass): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in result count is not announced to screen reader users. The function updates the count badge visually but does not use a live region to communicate the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches)): `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in the faqAccordion module animate the FAQ panels open and closed but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons dynamically. This violates the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern which requires the expanded state to be communicated to assistive technologies when the state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open after item.open = true): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line (in close after item.open = false): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price changes are updated visually but not announced to screen reader users. The function should announce the new billing period to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _syncToggleState(period)): `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation toggle button when the page loads. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate its initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init after _initialised = true): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function in dropdownMenu sets up dropdown triggers but does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute to "false" when the panel is hidden by default. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation valid state not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false" when the field becomes valid. This leaves stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid after describedby cleanup): `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls, but the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" when the suggestions list is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in mount after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox')): `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen reader users. While aria-expanded is required, a live region announcement provides additional confirmation of the action, especially when multiple items can be open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} expanded`);`
  Line (in close after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer should be announced to screen reader users to confirm the action, particularly because the focus management may not always be obvious to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line (in close after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus should be announced to provide feedback to screen reader users, particularly when the menu content changes dynamically or when the user is navigating via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line (in _closeInstance after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility not setting aria-invalid on errors
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid(): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger button missing aria-expanded state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions open() and close() add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the visibility state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of the function. Line close(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close dropdown panels do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. The attribute is initialised in register() but never updated when the state changes, so screen readers will incorrectly announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line _closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it finds the trigger button but does not set aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the drawer is initially closed. This prevents screen readers from understanding the initial state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions animate the panel height and set the open property, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers will not announce whether the accordion item is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line close(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is shown and hidden by these functions, but the aria-expanded attribute on the combobox input is never updated. Screen readers will not announce when the suggestion list opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line _open(): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line _close(): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the search combobox is mounted, the input element is given role="combobox" and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false". This prevents screen readers from knowing the initial collapsed state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line mount(): After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field transitions from invalid to valid, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid(): After removing aria-describedby, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The code declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never instantiates it with createFocusTrap. When the drawer opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and focus is not trapped within the drawer while it is open. Additionally, when closing, focus is not returned to the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): After `_lockScroll();`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate();`. Line close(): Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (it is already called but _trap is always null).

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product cards update and the count badge changes, but there is no announcement via a live region to inform screen reader users how many results are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters(): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realise the prices have changed or which period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod(): At the end of the function after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function accepts a returnFocus parameter, but it never implements the logic to actually return focus to the trigger button when closing the dropdown. Keyboard users will lose focus position when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line _closeInstance(): At the end of the function, add `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) { setTimeout(() => inst.trigger.focus(), 0); }`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users will lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line close(): After `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced via a live region. While focus moves to the panel content when opened, the collapse action is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`. Line close(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens or closes, no announcement is made via a live region. Although focus management should handle this, an explicit announcement ensures users understand the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line close(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Users may not be aware that the menu has appeared or disappeared, especially if focus did not move into the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened');`. Line _closeInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions (`open` and `close`) modify the visibility of the drawer and overlay but fail to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller functions (`_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`) show and hide the panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). This prevents assistive technology from conveying the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input does not toggle aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search component functions (`_open` and `_close`) manage the suggestion list visibility but do not toggle `aria-expanded` on the search input element (`_inputEl`). This violates the ARIA combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: siteSearch._open function: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion `open` function sets `aria-expanded` to 'false' in the `close` function but fails to set it to 'true' when opening. This leaves the state inconsistent for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open function: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as a radio group (single selection) but the synchronization function only toggles the CSS `active` class. It does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute to reflect the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons function as a radio group but the synchronization function only toggles the CSS `active` class. It does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute to reflect the selected period.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._syncToggleState function: Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the text content and `aria-label` of the product count badge when filters change, but it does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter._updateCountBadge function: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the text content of the savings message element when the billing period changes, but it does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the updated savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._updateSavingsMessage function: Add `liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);` after `_savingsMsg.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function `markInvalid` links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to identify invalid inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid function: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function `markValid` removes the error description but fails to set `aria-invalid="false"` (or remove the attribute) on the field. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid function: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the start of the function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization function sets up event listeners but does not initialize the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). The initial state (closed) is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.init function: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown registration function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The initial closed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu.register function: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search listbox mounting function sets `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup` on the input but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"`. The initial closed state of the suggestion list is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: suggestionListRenderer.mount function: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab click action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab updates the product grid and count, but the action result is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter._onTabClick function: After `applyFilters();`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Filter applied: ${value}`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period button click action not announced via live region

issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._onPeriodBtnClick function: After `setPeriod(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period changed to ${period}`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form input references non-existent error ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in NewsletterForm has aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but no element with this ID is rendered in the DOM (FormGroup does not receive errorId). Screen readers announce a reference that leads nowhere.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />
  (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.fix: Line 1273: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element containing skip links is a landmark region but lacks an `aria-label` to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 263: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element inside the mobile navigation panel lacks an `aria-label`. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, this landmark needs a unique name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 781: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is rendered as text without `aria-hidden`. Screen readers may announce "star" or "asterisk" unnecessarily, as the required state is already conveyed programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 396: *

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span rendered inside the button during loading state is decorative but lacks `aria-hidden`. It may be announced to screen readers, creating noise alongside the `loadingLabel`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The button element does not receive `aria-busy={isLoading}` when in a loading state. Assistive technology users are not informed that the button is temporarily unavailable due to processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual ring span inside the `Spinner` component is decorative but lacks `aria-hidden`. The text label and `role="status"` provide the accessible information, so the visual element should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 359: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link uses a `current` prop to apply a visual "current" class but lacks `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 693: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link uses a `current` prop to apply a visual "current" class but lacks `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 807: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div>` containing the trust badges acts as a group of related information but lacks an `aria-label`. Screen reader users may not understand the context of this group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges and certifications">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop overlay is purely decorative and used for click-outside closing. It should have `aria-hidden="true"` to prevent it from interfering with screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} />

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned list of integrations is used for visual looping only and duplicates content already available in the first list. It must have `aria-hidden="true"` to prevent duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1066: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee animation is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second ul element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons indicate selection visually but do not communicate the pressed state to assistive technology via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button indicates selection visually but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button indicates selection visually but lacks aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative icon not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative chevron icon inside the dropdown trigger is not hidden from assistive technology and may be announced as text content.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the chevron.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation landmark lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link is styled with a distinct class but lacks aria-current="page" to communicate the current location to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile menu lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button manages isOpen state but does not communicate the menu visibility to screen readers via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region containing a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section lacks aria-labelledby to provide an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the "‹" character and lacks an aria-label, making its purpose unclear to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the "›" character and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: Button spinner not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner inside the button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, potentially causing redundant announcements alongside the loadingLabel text.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span with className="btn-spinner".

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button component accepts an isLoading state but does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-busy={isLoading ? "true" : undefined} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visible asterisk marking required fields is announced by screen readers as "star" rather than being purely decorative, though a visually hidden "(required)" text is also present.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the asterisk.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel contains only the "✕" character and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button controlling the navigation submenu has aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses mobile navigation sections tracks isExpanded state but does not expose it via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls a panel but does not communicate the panel's visibility state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling error states but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, preventing screen readers from perceiving error states.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop but does not communicate the error state to assistive technology via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop but does not expose the error state to screen readers via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the select element.

issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error ? 'true' : 'false'} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input field lacks aria-invalid when in an error state, preventing screen readers from announcing the validation failure.

'true' : 'false'} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation element is a <nav> without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when multiple nav elements exist on the page.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing descriptive accessible name

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating container has an aria-label describing the value but lacks role="img", so assistive technology may not treat it as a single image/graphic and may not announce the label correctly.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator (" *") is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" after the label text even though the required status is already conveyed via the "(required)" visually-hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: *

issuesFound.title: Button spinner not hidden from AT

issuesFound.title: Mobile accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
